plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Trim CRWD to 10% and SMH to 8% to reduce single name concentration and downtrending exposure. Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad index exposure. Buy AMD at 5% as a top-quality long setup.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month outlook with significantly reduced risk. It aims for low drawdown by cutting leverage and high concentration, but potential upside is also limited.

moves.note: Reduce concentration in a single name

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce gross leverage. Sell SOXL to remove the flagged position. Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure. Trim CRWD to 12% and SMH to 10% to reduce concentration and downtrending exposure. Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income and broad index mix.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month by blending broad index, quality tech, and reduced leverage. It aims for risk-first growth with moderate drawdowns, suitable for a balanced approach.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits. Sell SOXL to exit the flagged position and reallocate. Trim SPAL to 2% to manage leveraged ETF exposure. Increase CRWD to 16% to press a winning AI/Software position. Buy AMD at 10% as a top-scoring chip setup. Buy RDDT at 5% as a high risk/reward long setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage. Be aware this raises both potential upside and drawdown significantly, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and shift focus to income. Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk. Significantly reduce CRWD to 5% and sell SMH to exit high-beta and downtrending positions. Maximize QQQI to 35% for income generation. Increase QQQ to 20% to maintain broad index stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month by rotating into income/covered-call ETFs and broad index. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable distributions over growth. Note that distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
